title: Implement needle setup command
description:
## Overview
Implement the `needle setup` command that checks and installs dependencies.

## Background & Rationale
The setup command is the user-facing interface for dependency management. It:
- Checks if all dependencies are present and up-to-date
- Optionally installs missing dependencies
- Provides clear feedback on what's installed/missing

## Technical Requirements

### Command Options
```
needle setup [OPTIONS]

OPTIONS:
    -c, --check       Check only, don't install anything
    -r, --reinstall   Force reinstall all dependencies
    -y, --yes         Don't prompt for confirmation
    -h, --help        Print help information
```

### Implementation (cli/setup.sh)
```bash
_needle_setup() {
    local check_only=false
    local reinstall=false
    local auto_yes=false
    
    while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
        case "$1" in
            -c|--check) check_only=true; shift ;;
            -r|--reinstall) reinstall=true; shift ;;
            -y|--yes) auto_yes=true; shift ;;
            -h|--help) _needle_setup_help; return 0 ;;
            *) _needle_error "Unknown option: $1"; return 1 ;;
        esac
    done
    
    _needle_info "Checking dependencies..."
    
    local missing=()
    local installed=()
    
    for dep in tmux jq yq br; do
        if command -v "$dep" &>/dev/null && ! $reinstall; then
            local ver=$(_needle_parse_version "$dep")
            _needle_success "$dep $ver"
            installed+=("$dep")
        else
            _needle_warn "$dep not found"
            missing+=("$dep")
        fi
    done
    
    if [[ ${#missing[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
        _needle_success "All dependencies installed!"
        return 0
    fi
    
    if $check_only; then
        _needle_error "Missing: ${missing[*]}"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Install missing
    if ! $auto_yes; then
        read -p "Install missing dependencies? [Y/n] " -n 1 -r
        echo
        [[ $REPLY =~ ^[Nn]$ ]] && return 1
    fi
    
    for dep in "${missing[@]}"; do
        _needle_info "Installing $dep..."
        "_needle_install_$dep"
    done
    
    _needle_success "Setup complete!"
}
```

### Output Format
```
$ needle setup

Checking dependencies...
  ✓ tmux 3.4
  ✓ jq 1.7
  ⚠ yq not found
  ⚠ br not found

Install missing dependencies? [Y/n] y

Installing yq...
  ↓ Downloading from github.com/mikefarah/yq...
  ✓ yq 4.40 installed to ~/.needle/cache/

Installing br...
  ↓ Downloading from github.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_rust...
  ✓ br 0.8.0 installed to ~/.needle/cache/

✓ Setup complete!
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `needle setup` checks all dependencies
- [ ] `needle setup --check` exits 1 if missing, 0 if ok
- [ ] `needle setup` prompts before installing
- [ ] `needle setup --yes` installs without prompt
- [ ] `needle setup --reinstall` reinstalls everything
- [ ] Clear progress indicators during install
- [ ] Appropriate exit codes

## Dependencies
- nd-n0y (dependency installation)
- nd-39i (dependency detection)

## Labels
phase-1, cli, bootstrap

title: Implement bead mitosis module (bead/mitosis.sh)
description:
## Overview
Implement automatic bead decomposition for complex tasks.

## Background & Rationale
Large beads are the #1 cause of agent failure. Mitosis:
- Detects when a bead represents multiple tasks
- Splits into child beads with dependencies
- Parent auto-completes when children finish
- Enables parallel work and better success rates

## Technical Requirements

### Detection Heuristics
| Heuristic | Threshold | Action |
|-----------|-----------|--------|
| File count | >5 files | Split by file/group |
| Unrelated concerns | Multiple tasks | Split by concern |
| Explicit markers | "and", numbered items | Split at boundaries |
| Size estimate | >500 lines | Split into phases |

### Implementation
```bash
_needle_check_mitosis() {
    local bead_id="$1"
    local workspace="$2"
    local agent="$3"
    
    local enabled=$(_needle_config_get "mitosis.enabled" "true")
    [[ "$enabled" != "true" ]] && return 1
    
    # Get bead details
    local bead_json=$(br show "$bead_id" --json)
    local bead_type=$(echo "$bead_json" | jq -r '.type // "task"')
    local labels=$(echo "$bead_json" | jq -r '.labels // [] | join(",")')
    
    # Skip certain types
    local skip_types=$(_needle_config_get "mitosis.skip_types" "bug,hotfix")
    if [[ "$skip_types" == *"$bead_type"* ]]; then
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Skip if labeled no-mitosis
    if [[ "$labels" == *"no-mitosis"* || "$labels" == *"atomic"* ]]; then
        return 1
    fi
    
    _needle_emit_event "bead.mitosis.check" \
        "bead_id=$bead_id"
    
    # Build analysis prompt
    local prompt=$(_needle_build_mitosis_prompt "$bead_id" "$workspace")
    
    # Run quick analysis (use same agent)
    local result=$(_needle_dispatch_agent "$agent" "$workspace" "$prompt" "$bead_id" "mitosis-check")
    IFS='|' read -r exit_code duration output_file <<< "$result"
    
    # Parse result
    local analysis=$(cat "$output_file")
    rm -f "$output_file"
    
    local should_split=$(echo "$analysis" | jq -r '.mitosis // false')
    
    if [[ "$should_split" == "true" ]]; then
        _needle_perform_mitosis "$bead_id" "$workspace" "$analysis"
        return 0  # Mitosis performed, don't process original
    fi
    
    return 1  # No mitosis needed
}

_needle_perform_mitosis() {
    local parent_id="$1"
    local workspace="$2"
    local analysis="$3"
    
    local children=$(echo "$analysis" | jq -c '.children[]')
    local child_ids=()
    local prev_id=""
    
    while IFS= read -r child; do
        local title=$(echo "$child" | jq -r '.title')
        local description=$(echo "$child" | jq -r '.description')
        local blocked_by=$(echo "$child" | jq -r '.blocked_by // [] | join(",")')
        
        # Create child bead
        local child_id=$(br create "$title" \
            --workspace="$workspace" \
            --description "$description" \
            --priority 2 \
            --type task \
            --label "mitosis-child" \
            --label "parent-$parent_id" \
            --json | jq -r '.id')
        
        child_ids+=("$child_id")
        
        # Set blocking relationship if needed
        if [[ -n "$prev_id" && "$blocked_by" == *"previous"* ]]; then
            br update "$child_id" --blocked-by "$prev_id"
        fi
        
        prev_id="$child_id"
        
        _needle_emit_event "bead.mitosis.child_created" \
            "parent_id=$parent_id" \
            "child_id=$child_id" \
            "title=$title"
    done <<< "$children"
    
    # Mark parent as blocked by all children
    for child_id in "${child_ids[@]}"; do
        br update "$parent_id" --blocked-by "$child_id"
    done
    
    # Release our claim on parent (children will be worked instead)
    br update "$parent_id" --release --reason "mitosis"
    
    # Add mitosis label to parent
    br update "$parent_id" --label "mitosis-parent"
    
    _needle_emit_event "bead.mitosis.complete" \
        "parent_id=$parent_id" \
        "children=${child_ids[*]}"
}
```

### Mitosis Prompt
As documented in plan.md - analyzes bead and returns JSON with:
- `mitosis: true/false`
- `children: [{title, description, blocked_by}]`
- `reasoning: "why split"`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Detects complex beads needing split
- [ ] Respects skip_types config
- [ ] Respects no-mitosis label
- [ ] Creates child beads with correct dependencies
- [ ] Sets parent blocked by children
- [ ] Releases claim on parent
- [ ] Emits all mitosis events
- [ ] Children can be worked in parallel

## Dependencies
- nd-hpj (strand engine)
- nd-1hr (agent dispatcher)
- nd-31l (bead management)

## Labels
phase-3, bead, mitosis

title: Implement dependency detection module (bootstrap/check.sh)
description:
## Overview
Create the dependency detection module that checks for required tools.

## Background & Rationale
Before NEEDLE can run, it needs to verify that all required dependencies are available:
- tmux (3.0+) - Session management
- jq (1.6+) - JSON parsing
- yq (4.0+) - YAML parsing  
- br (latest) - Beads queue management

This module provides the detection logic used by both `needle setup --check` and auto-initialization.

## Technical Requirements

### Dependency Specification
```bash
# Define minimum versions
declare -A NEEDLE_DEPS=(
    [tmux]="3.0"
    [jq]="1.6"
    [yq]="4.0"
    [br]="0.1"
)
```

### Version Parsing
```bash
_needle_parse_version() {
    local cmd="$1"
    case "$cmd" in
        tmux) tmux -V 2>/dev/null | grep -oE '[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' ;;
        jq)   jq --version 2>/dev/null | grep -oE '[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' ;;
        yq)   yq --version 2>/dev/null | grep -oE '[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' ;;
        br)   br --version 2>/dev/null | grep -oE '[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' ;;
    esac
}
```

### Version Comparison
```bash
_needle_version_gte() {
    local have="$1" need="$2"
    printf '%s\n%s' "$need" "$have" | sort -V | head -1 | grep -qx "$need"
}
```

### Check All Dependencies
```bash
_needle_check_deps() {
    local missing=() outdated=()
    
    for dep in "${!NEEDLE_DEPS[@]}"; do
        if ! command -v "$dep" &>/dev/null; then
            missing+=("$dep")
        else
            local ver=$(_needle_parse_version "$dep")
            local min="${NEEDLE_DEPS[$dep]}"
            if ! _needle_version_gte "$ver" "$min"; then
                outdated+=("$dep ($ver < $min)")
            fi
        fi
    done
    
    # Return results
    if [[ ${#missing[@]} -gt 0 || ${#outdated[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
        return 1
    fi
    return 0
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Detects presence of tmux, jq, yq, br
- [ ] Parses version numbers correctly for each tool
- [ ] Compares versions accurately (handles 1.10 > 1.9)
- [ ] Reports missing dependencies clearly
- [ ] Reports outdated dependencies with version info
- [ ] Returns appropriate exit code (0=ok, 1=missing)

## Dependencies
- nd-1il (shared libraries)

## Labels
phase-1, bootstrap, dependencies

title: Implement needle test-agent command (cli/test-agent.sh)
description:
## Overview
Implement the `needle test-agent` command for testing agent adapter configurations.

## Technical Requirements

### Command Options
```
needle test-agent <AGENT> [OPTIONS]

OPTIONS:
    -p, --prompt <TEXT>   Custom test prompt
    -t, --timeout <SECS>  Timeout for test
    -v, --verbose         Show full agent output
    -h, --help            Print help
```

### Implementation
```bash
_needle_test_agent() {
    local agent=""
    local prompt="echo 'Hello from NEEDLE test'"
    local timeout=60
    local verbose=false
    
    while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
        case "$1" in
            -p|--prompt) prompt="$2"; shift 2 ;;
            -t|--timeout) timeout="$2"; shift 2 ;;
            -v|--verbose) verbose=true; shift ;;
            -h|--help) _needle_test_agent_help; return 0 ;;
            -*) _needle_error "Unknown option: $1"; return 1 ;;
            *) agent="$1"; shift ;;
        esac
    done
    
    if [[ -z "$agent" ]]; then
        _needle_error "Agent name required"
        return 1
    fi
    
    _needle_info "Testing agent: $agent"
    echo ""
    
    # Test 1: Agent config exists
    echo -n "1. Agent config exists... "
    if ! _needle_load_agent "$agent" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "FAIL"
        _needle_error "Agent configuration not found"
        return 1
    fi
    echo "OK"
    
    # Test 2: Runner exists
    echo -n "2. Runner '${NEEDLE_AGENT[runner]}' in PATH... "
    if ! command -v "${NEEDLE_AGENT[runner]}" &>/dev/null; then
        echo "FAIL"
        _needle_error "Runner not found: ${NEEDLE_AGENT[runner]}"
        return 1
    fi
    echo "OK"
    
    # Test 3: Invoke template renders
    echo -n "3. Invoke template renders... "
    local rendered=$(_needle_render_invoke \
        "${NEEDLE_AGENT[invoke]}" \
        "/tmp" \
        "$prompt" \
        "test-bead" \
        "Test bead"
    )
    if [[ -z "$rendered" ]]; then
        echo "FAIL"
        return 1
    fi
    echo "OK"
    
    # Test 4: Agent executes
    echo -n "4. Agent executes successfully... "
    local output_file=$(mktemp)
    local exit_code
    
    if timeout "$timeout" bash -c "$rendered" > "$output_file" 2>&1; then
        exit_code=0
        echo "OK"
    else
        exit_code=$?
        echo "FAIL (exit code: $exit_code)"
    fi
    
    if $verbose; then
        echo ""
        echo "--- Output ---"
        cat "$output_file"
        echo "--- End ---"
    fi
    
    rm -f "$output_file"
    
    # Summary
    echo ""
    if [[ $exit_code -eq 0 ]]; then
        _needle_success "Agent '$agent' is working correctly"
        return 0
    else
        _needle_error "Agent '$agent' test failed"
        return 1
    fi
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Validates agent config exists
- [ ] Validates runner in PATH
- [ ] Tests template rendering
- [ ] Executes test prompt
- [ ] Respects timeout
- [ ] --verbose shows output
- [ ] Clear pass/fail reporting

- nd-3pu (agent loader)
- nd-1hr (agent dispatcher)

title: Implement worker loop: Cleanup and crash recovery
description:
## Overview
Part 3 of 3: Cleanup after execution and crash recovery with backoff.

## Parent Bead
Split from nd-qft (too large - combined loop, execution, cleanup, recovery)

## Scope
Handle execution results, cleanup on success/failure, crash recovery.

## Technical Requirements

### Exit Code Handling
```bash
handle_exit_code() {
    local bead_id="$1"
    local exit_code="$2"
    
    case $exit_code in
        0)
            # Success - close bead
            br close "$bead_id" --reason "completed"
            emit_event bead_completed "$bead_id"
            reset_backoff
            ;;
        1)
            # Failure - release and retry later
            br update "$bead_id" --release
            emit_event bead_failed "$bead_id"
            increment_backoff
            ;;
        124)
            # Timeout - release and flag
            br update "$bead_id" --release --label timeout
            emit_event bead_timeout "$bead_id"
            ;;
        *)
            # Unknown error - release
            br update "$bead_id" --release
            emit_event bead_error "$bead_id" $exit_code
            increment_backoff
            ;;
    esac
}
```

### Crash Recovery
- Detect repeated failures (3+ in a row)
- Exponential backoff: 30s, 60s, 120s
- Eventually pause worker and alert human
- Use nd-d2a (Strand 7: Knot) for alerting

### Cleanup
- Release bead on failure
- Update worker state
- Emit telemetry events
- Clear execution context

### Backoff Logic
```bash
FAILURE_COUNT=0
BACKOFF_SECONDS=30

increment_backoff() {
    ((FAILURE_COUNT++))
    BACKOFF_SECONDS=0
    
    if [[ $FAILURE_COUNT -ge 3 ]]; then
        sleep $BACKOFF_SECONDS
    fi
    
    if [[ $FAILURE_COUNT -ge 5 ]]; then
        alert_human "Worker failing repeatedly"
        exit 1
    fi
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Handles all exit codes correctly
- [ ] Closes bead on success
- [ ] Releases bead on failure
- [ ] Implements exponential backoff
- [ ] Detects crash loops
- [ ] Alerts human on repeated failures
- [ ] Cleans up execution state

title: Implement needle logs command (cli/logs.sh)
description:
## Overview
Implement the `needle logs` command for viewing worker logs.

## Technical Requirements

### Command Options
As documented in plan.md needle logs --help section.

### Implementation
```bash
_needle_logs() {
    local worker=""
    local follow=false
    local lines=50
    local since=""
    local until=""
    local event_filter=""
    local bead_filter=""
    local strand_filter=""
    local raw=false
    local json_output=false
    
    # Parse options...
    
    # Determine log files to read
    local log_files=()
    
    if [[ -n "$worker" ]]; then
        log_files=("$NEEDLE_LOGS/needle-*-$worker.jsonl")
    else
        log_files=("$NEEDLE_LOGS"/*.jsonl)
    fi
    
    # Build jq filter
    local jq_filter="."
    [[ -n "$event_filter" ]] && jq_filter+=" | select(.event == \"$event_filter\")"
    [[ -n "$bead_filter" ]] && jq_filter+=" | select(.data.bead_id == \"$bead_filter\")"
    [[ -n "$strand_filter" ]] && jq_filter+=" | select(.data.strand == $strand_filter)"
    
    if $follow; then
        # Tail with follow
        tail -f "${log_files[@]}" 2>/dev/null | while IFS= read -r line; do
            if $raw; then
                echo "$line"
            else
                _needle_format_log_line "$line"
            fi
        done
    else
        # Static read
        cat "${log_files[@]}" 2>/dev/null | \
            jq -c "$jq_filter" 2>/dev/null | \
            tail -n "$lines" | \
            if $raw; then cat; else while IFS= read -r line; do _needle_format_log_line "$line"; done; fi
    fi
}

_needle_format_log_line() {
    local line="$1"
    local ts=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.ts' | cut -c12-19)
    local event=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.event')
    local session=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.session' | sed 's/needle-//')
    
    # Color by event type
    local color=""
    case "$event" in
        *.completed) color="\033[32m" ;;  # green
        *.failed|error.*) color="\033[31m" ;;  # red
        *.started) color="\033[34m" ;;  # blue
        *) color="" ;;
    esac
    
    printf "%s %b%-20s\033[0m [%s] %s\n" \
        "$ts" \
        "$color" \
        "$event" \
        "$session" \
        "$(echo "$line" | jq -c '.data')"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Shows logs for all workers
- [ ] Filters by worker identifier
- [ ] --follow tails in real-time
- [ ] --lines limits output
- [ ] --event filters by event type
- [ ] --bead filters by bead ID
- [ ] --since/--until time filtering
- [ ] --raw shows unformatted JSONL
- [ ] Colored output for event types

## Dependencies
- nd-1cz (telemetry events)
- nd-1il (shared libraries)

## Labels
phase-1, cli, logs

title: Implement needle upgrade command (cli/upgrade.sh)
description:
## Overview
Implement the `needle upgrade` command for self-updating.

## Technical Requirements

As documented in plan.md Self-Update System section:
- Check GitHub releases for new version
- Download with checksum verification
- Atomic binary swap with backup
- Rollback support

### Implementation
```bash
_needle_upgrade() {
    local check_only=false
    local force=false
    local yes=false
    local version="latest"
    local local_file=""
    local list=false
    
    # Parse options...
    
    if $list; then
        _needle_upgrade_list
        return
    fi
    
    if $check_only; then
        _needle_upgrade_check
        return
    fi
    
    # Get latest version
    local latest=$(_needle_get_latest_version)
    local current="$NEEDLE_VERSION"
    
    if [[ "$latest" == "$current" ]] && ! $force; then
        _needle_info "Already at latest version ($current)"
        return 0
    fi
    
    _needle_info "Current version: $current"
    _needle_info "Latest version:  $latest"
    
    # Confirm
    if ! $yes; then
        read -p "Proceed with upgrade? [Y/n] " -n 1 -r
        echo
        [[ $REPLY =~ ^[Nn]$ ]] && return 0
    fi
    
    # Download
    _needle_upgrade_download "$latest"
    
    # Swap
    _needle_upgrade_swap
    
    _needle_success "Upgraded to $latest"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] --check shows available update
- [ ] Downloads new binary
- [ ] Verifies checksum
- [ ] Creates backup
- [ ] Atomic swap
- [ ] --rollback works
- [ ] --local for air-gapped

## Dependencies
- nd-1il (shared libraries)

phase-1, cli, upgrade

title: Implement agent adapter YAML schema and loader (agent/loader.sh)
description:
## Overview
Create the agent adapter configuration schema and loader that reads agent YAML files.

## Background & Rationale
NEEDLE's core value proposition is agent-agnostic execution. The adapter system allows:
- Adding new agents with YAML config only (no code changes)
- Customizing invocation per agent
- Different input methods (stdin, file, args)
- Token extraction patterns

This is one of the most important components for extensibility.

## Technical Requirements

### Agent YAML Schema
```yaml
# config/agents/claude-anthropic-sonnet.yaml

# Agent metadata
name: claude-anthropic-sonnet
description: Claude Code with Anthropic Sonnet model
version: "1.0"

# CLI identification
runner: claude          # CLI executable name
provider: anthropic     # Provider for rate limiting
model: sonnet          # Model identifier

# Invocation template
# Variables: ${WORKSPACE}, ${PROMPT}, ${BEAD_ID}, ${BEAD_TITLE}
invoke: |
  cd ${WORKSPACE} && \
  ANTHROPIC_MODEL=claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929 \
  claude --print \
         --dangerously-skip-permissions \
         --output-format json \
         --verbose \
  <<'NEEDLE_PROMPT'
  ${PROMPT}
  NEEDLE_PROMPT

# Input method
input:
  method: heredoc      # heredoc | stdin | file | args
  # For file method:
  # file_path: /tmp/needle-prompt-${BEAD_ID}.txt
  # For args method:
  # arg_flag: --message

# Output parsing
output:
  format: json         # json | text | structured
  # Token extraction regex (if not JSON)
  token_pattern: "tokens_used: (\\d+)"
  # Exit code interpretation
  success_codes: [0]
  retry_codes: [1]
  fail_codes: [2, 137]

# Rate limits (can override global)
limits:
  requests_per_minute: 60
  max_concurrent: 5
```

### Loader Implementation
```bash
# agent/loader.sh

_needle_load_agent() {
    local agent_name="$1"
    local agent_file
    
    # Search order: workspace, user, builtin
    for dir in ".needle/agents" "$NEEDLE_HOME/agents" "$NEEDLE_BUILTIN_AGENTS"; do
        agent_file="$dir/${agent_name}.yaml"
        [[ -f "$agent_file" ]] && break
    done
    
    if [[ ! -f "$agent_file" ]]; then
        _needle_error "Agent not found: $agent_name"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Load into associative array
    declare -gA NEEDLE_AGENT
    NEEDLE_AGENT[name]=$(yq '.name' "$agent_file")
    NEEDLE_AGENT[runner]=$(yq '.runner' "$agent_file")
    NEEDLE_AGENT[provider]=$(yq '.provider' "$agent_file")
    NEEDLE_AGENT[model]=$(yq '.model' "$agent_file")
    NEEDLE_AGENT[invoke]=$(yq '.invoke' "$agent_file")
    NEEDLE_AGENT[input_method]=$(yq '.input.method' "$agent_file")
    NEEDLE_AGENT[output_format]=$(yq '.output.format' "$agent_file")
}

_needle_validate_agent() {
    local agent_name="$1"
    
    _needle_load_agent "$agent_name" || return 1
    
    # Check runner exists
    if ! command -v "${NEEDLE_AGENT[runner]}" &>/dev/null; then
        _needle_error "Runner not found: ${NEEDLE_AGENT[runner]}"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Validate invoke template
    if [[ -z "${NEEDLE_AGENT[invoke]}" ]]; then
        _needle_error "Missing invoke template"
        return 1
    fi
    
    return 0
}
```

### Built-in Agent Configs
Create configs for:
- claude-anthropic-sonnet.yaml
- claude-anthropic-opus.yaml
- opencode-alibaba-qwen.yaml
- opencode-ollama-deepseek.yaml
- codex-openai-gpt4.yaml
- aider-ollama-deepseek.yaml

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] YAML schema documented and validated
- [ ] Loader reads agent config from multiple paths
- [ ] Search order: workspace → user → builtin
- [ ] All agent properties accessible
- [ ] Validation catches missing runner
- [ ] Validation catches missing invoke template
- [ ] Built-in configs for all supported agents
- [ ] yq errors handled gracefully

## Dependencies
- nd-1il (shared libraries)

## Labels
phase-1, agent, adapter

title: Implement needle init command (interactive onboarding)
description:
## Overview
Implement the `needle init` command that guides users through first-time setup.

## Background & Rationale
The init command is critical for user experience. It:
- Installs dependencies automatically
- Detects available agents
- Sets up the first workspace
- Creates sensible default configuration
- Makes NEEDLE immediately usable after completion

This is also auto-invoked when any command runs in an unconfigured environment.

## Technical Requirements

### Command Options
```
needle init [OPTIONS]

OPTIONS:
    -f, --force              Overwrite existing configuration
    -c, --check              Check if initialization is needed (exit 0=yes, 1=no)
    -n, --non-interactive    Run without prompts (use defaults)
    --workspace <PATH>       Preset workspace path
    --agent <NAME>           Preset agent name
    -h, --help               Print help information
```

### Onboarding Steps

**Step 1: Dependencies**
```bash
_needle_init_deps() {
    echo "Step 1/4: Installing dependencies..."
    echo ""
    
    for dep in tmux jq yq br; do
        if command -v "$dep" &>/dev/null; then
            local ver=$(_needle_parse_version "$dep")
            _needle_success "$dep $ver (already installed)"
        else
            _needle_info "$dep (installing...)"
            "_needle_install_$dep"
            _needle_success "$dep installed"
        fi
    done
    
    echo ""
    echo "    All dependencies installed!"
    echo ""
}
```

**Step 2: Agent Detection**
```bash
_needle_init_agents() {
    echo "Step 2/4: Detecting available agents..."
    echo ""
    echo "    Scanning PATH for coding CLIs..."
    echo ""
    
    _needle_scan_agents
}
```

**Step 3: Workspace Setup**
```bash
_needle_init_workspace() {
    echo "Step 3/4: Configure your first workspace..."
    echo ""
    
    local workspace
    if [[ -n "${preset_workspace:-}" ]]; then
        workspace="$preset_workspace"
    elif $non_interactive; then
        workspace="$(pwd)"
    else
        echo "    Enter workspace path (or press Enter for current directory):"
        read -p "    > " workspace
        workspace="${workspace:-$(pwd)}"
    fi
    
    # Validate workspace
    if [[ ! -d "$workspace" ]]; then
        _needle_error "Directory not found: $workspace"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Check for beads workspace
    if [[ -d "$workspace/.beads" ]]; then
        local count=$(br list --workspace="$workspace" --count 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
        _needle_success "Found .beads/ with $count open issues"
    else
        _needle_warn "No .beads/ directory found"
        echo "    Run 'br init' in your workspace to create one."
    fi
}
```

**Step 4: Create Config**
```bash
_needle_init_config() {
    echo "Step 4/4: Create default configuration..."
    echo ""
    
    _needle_ensure_dirs
    
    cat > "$NEEDLE_CONFIG" <<YAML
# NEEDLE Configuration
# Generated by needle init on $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)

# Default workspaces to process
workspaces:
  - $workspace

# Default agent
default_agent: $selected_agent

# Runner settings
runner:
  polling_interval: 2s
  idle_timeout: 300s
  max_consecutive_empty: 5

# Strand settings
strands:
  pluck: true
  explore: true
  mend: true
  weave: false
  unravel: false
  pulse: false
  knot: true

# Heartbeat settings
heartbeat:
  interval: 30s
  timeout: 120s
  bead_timeout: 600s
  recovery:
    enabled: true
    action: restart
YAML

    _needle_success "Configuration created"
}
```

### Completion Message
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                     Setup Complete!                         │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                             │
│  Start your first worker:                                   │
│                                                             │
│    needle run                                               │
│                                                             │
│  Useful commands:                                           │
```

title: Implement worker heartbeat emission (watchdog/heartbeat.sh)
description:
## Overview
Implement the heartbeat emission that workers use to signal they're alive.

## Background & Rationale
Heartbeats enable stuck detection:
- Workers emit heartbeats every 30s
- Watchdog monitors for missing heartbeats
- Stuck workers can be auto-recovered

## Technical Requirements

### Heartbeat File Format
```json
{
  "worker": "needle-claude-anthropic-sonnet-alpha",
  "pid": 12345,
  "started": "2026-03-01T10:00:00Z",
  "last_heartbeat": "2026-03-01T10:30:00Z",
  "status": "executing",
  "current_bead": "bd-123",
  "bead_started": "2026-03-01T10:25:00Z",
  "strand": 1,
  "workspace": "/home/coder/project",
  "agent": "claude-anthropic-sonnet"
}
```

### Implementation
```bash
# watchdog/heartbeat.sh

NEEDLE_HEARTBEAT_FILE=""
NEEDLE_HEARTBEAT_STARTED=""

_needle_heartbeat_init() {
    NEEDLE_HEARTBEAT_FILE="$NEEDLE_STATE/heartbeats/${NEEDLE_SESSION}.json"
    NEEDLE_HEARTBEAT_STARTED=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
    
    mkdir -p "$(dirname "$NEEDLE_HEARTBEAT_FILE")"
    
    _needle_emit_heartbeat "starting"
}

_needle_emit_heartbeat() {
    local status="${1:-idle}"
    local current_bead="${2:-}"
    local bead_started="${3:-}"
    local strand="${4:-}"
    
    jq -nc \
        --arg worker "$NEEDLE_SESSION" \
        --arg pid "$$" \
        --arg started "$NEEDLE_HEARTBEAT_STARTED" \
        --arg last_heartbeat "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)" \
        --arg status "$status" \
        --arg current_bead "$current_bead" \
        --arg bead_started "$bead_started" \
        --arg strand "$strand" \
        --arg workspace "$NEEDLE_WORKSPACE" \
        --arg agent "$NEEDLE_AGENT" \
        '{
            worker: $worker,
            pid: ($pid | tonumber),
            started: $started,
            last_heartbeat: $last_heartbeat,
            status: $status,
            current_bead: (if $current_bead == "" then null else $current_bead end),
            bead_started: (if $bead_started == "" then null else $bead_started end),
            strand: (if $strand == "" then null else ($strand | tonumber) end),
            workspace: $workspace,
            agent: $agent
        }' > "$NEEDLE_HEARTBEAT_FILE"
    
    _needle_emit_event "heartbeat.emitted" \
        "status=$status" \
        "current_bead=$current_bead"
}

_needle_heartbeat_start_bead() {
    local bead_id="$1"
    local strand="$2"
    
    _needle_emit_heartbeat "executing" "$bead_id" "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)" "$strand"
}

_needle_heartbeat_end_bead() {
    _needle_emit_heartbeat "idle"
}

_needle_heartbeat_draining() {
    _needle_emit_heartbeat "draining"
}

_needle_heartbeat_cleanup() {
    rm -f "$NEEDLE_HEARTBEAT_FILE"
}
```

### Integration with Worker Loop
```bash
# In runner/loop.sh

_needle_worker_loop() {
    _needle_heartbeat_init
    
    trap '_needle_heartbeat_cleanup' EXIT
    
    while true; do
        _needle_emit_heartbeat "idle"  # Periodic heartbeat
        
        # ... process bead ...
        
        _needle_heartbeat_start_bead "$bead_id" "$strand"
        # ... execute agent ...
        _needle_heartbeat_end_bead
    done
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Creates heartbeat file on init
- [ ] Updates heartbeat every cycle
- [ ] Tracks current bead and strand
- [ ] Tracks bead start time
- [ ] Updates status (idle/executing/draining)
- [ ] Cleans up file on exit
- [ ] Valid JSON format

## Dependencies
- nd-qft (worker loop)
- nd-1cz (telemetry)

## Labels
phase-2, watchdog, heartbeat

title: Implement strand engine dispatcher (strands/engine.sh)
description:
## Overview
Create the strand engine that dispatches work through the 7-strand system.

## Background & Rationale
The strand engine implements the priority waterfall. It DISPATCHES to strand implementations (in separate files) but does NOT implement the strands itself.

Strand order:
1. pluck - Primary work from configured workspaces (nd-2gc)
2. explore - Look for work in other workspaces (nd-hq2)
3. mend - Maintenance and cleanup (nd-1sk)
4. weave - Create beads from documentation gaps (nd-27u)
5. unravel - Create alternatives for blocked beads (nd-20p)
6. pulse - Codebase health monitoring (nd-qpj)
7. knot - Alert human when stuck (nd-d2a)

## Scope
This bead implements ONLY:
- The dispatch loop that iterates through strands
- Enabled/disabled checking per strand
- Telemetry events for strand transitions
- Return value handling (work_done vs no_work)

The actual strand logic is in separate beads (nd-2gc, nd-hq2, etc.).

## Technical Requirements

### Strand Dispatch (strands/engine.sh)
```bash
# Source individual strand implementations
source "$NEEDLE_SRC/strands/pluck.sh"
source "$NEEDLE_SRC/strands/explore.sh"
source "$NEEDLE_SRC/strands/mend.sh"
source "$NEEDLE_SRC/strands/weave.sh"
source "$NEEDLE_SRC/strands/unravel.sh"
source "$NEEDLE_SRC/strands/pulse.sh"
source "$NEEDLE_SRC/strands/knot.sh"

_needle_strand_engine() {
    local workspace="$1"
    local agent="$2"
    
    local strands=(pluck explore mend weave unravel pulse knot)
    local strand_num=1
    
    for strand in "${strands[@]}"; do
        # Check if strand enabled
        if ! _needle_is_strand_enabled "$strand"; then
            _needle_emit_event "strand.skipped" \
                "strand=$strand_num" "name=$strand" "reason=disabled"
            ((strand_num++))
            continue
        fi
        
        _needle_emit_event "strand.started" "strand=$strand_num" "name=$strand"
        
        # Dispatch to strand implementation (defined in separate files)
        local result
        "_needle_strand_$strand" "$workspace" "$agent"
        result=$?
        
        if [[ $result -eq 0 ]]; then
            _needle_emit_event "strand.completed" \
                "strand=$strand_num" "name=$strand" "result=work_found"
            return 0  # Work done
        fi
        
        # Fallthrough to next strand
        _needle_emit_event "strand.fallthrough" \
            "from=$strand_num" "from_name=$strand" "reason=no_work"
        
        ((strand_num++))
    done
    
    return 1  # No work found across all strands
}

_needle_is_strand_enabled() {
    local strand="$1"
    [[ "$(_needle_config_get "strands.$strand" "false")" == "true" ]]
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Iterates through strands 1-7 in order
- [ ] Respects strand enable/disable config
- [ ] Calls strand function (from sourced file)
- [ ] Emits strand.started, strand.completed, strand.skipped, strand.fallthrough
- [ ] Returns 0 if any strand found work
- [ ] Returns 1 if all strands exhausted

## Dependencies
- nd-1il (shared libs)
- nd-1cz (telemetry)

NOTE: Does NOT depend on individual strand beads - those are sourced at runtime.

## Labels
phase-1, strands, engine, dispatcher

title: Implement Strand 2: Explore (strands/explore.sh)
description:
## Overview
Implement Strand 2 (explore) that looks for work in other workspaces.

## Background & Rationale
When the primary workspace is exhausted, workers can explore parent directories to find other beads workspaces and spawn workers for them.

## Technical Requirements

### Exploration Logic
```bash
_needle_strand_explore() {
    local workspace="$1"
    local agent="$2"
    
    # Only explore after consecutive empty in primary workspace
    local explore_threshold=$(_needle_config_get "strands.explore.threshold" "3")
    
    # Traverse up to find other workspaces
    local search_dir=$(dirname "$workspace")
    local max_depth=3
    local depth=0
    
    while [[ "$search_dir" != "/" && $depth -lt $max_depth ]]; do
        # Look for .beads directories
        while IFS= read -r beads_dir; do
            local found_workspace=$(dirname "$beads_dir")
            
            # Skip if same as primary
            [[ "$found_workspace" == "$workspace" ]] && continue
            
            # Check for unassigned beads
            local count=$(br ready --workspace="$found_workspace" --unassigned --count 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
            
            if ((count > 0)); then
                _needle_emit_event "strand.explore.found" \
                    "workspace=$found_workspace" \
                    "bead_count=$count"
                
                # Spawn new worker for this workspace
                local spawn_threshold=$(_needle_config_get "scaling.spawn_threshold" "3")
                if ((count >= spawn_threshold)); then
                    _needle_spawn_worker "$found_workspace" "$agent"
                fi
            fi
        done < <(find "$search_dir" -maxdepth 2 -name ".beads" -type d 2>/dev/null)
        
        search_dir=$(dirname "$search_dir")
        ((depth++))
    done
    
    # Explore doesn't process beads itself
    return 1  # No work found (by this strand)
}

_needle_spawn_worker() {
    local workspace="$1"
    local agent="$2"
    
    local max_workers=$(_needle_config_get "scaling.max_workers_per_agent" "10")
    local current=$(needle list --agent="$agent" --count 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
    
    if ((current < max_workers)); then
        needle run --workspace="$workspace" --agent="$agent" &
        _needle_emit_event "strand.explore.spawned" \
            "workspace=$workspace" \
            "agent=$agent"
    fi
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Searches parent directories for .beads
- [ ] Respects max_depth limit
- [ ] Skips primary workspace
- [ ] Counts unassigned beads
- [ ] Spawns workers when threshold met
- [ ] Respects max_workers limit
- [ ] Emits discovery events
- [ ] Does not process beads itself

## Dependencies
- nd-hpj (strand engine)
- nd-qzu (needle run)

## Labels
phase-2, strands, explore

title: Implement dependency installation module (bootstrap/install.sh)
description:
## Overview
Create the dependency installation module that can automatically install missing tools.

## Background & Rationale
NEEDLE should be able to bootstrap itself by installing missing dependencies. This makes the one-liner install experience seamless. The module detects the OS and package manager, then installs each dependency appropriately.

## Technical Requirements

### OS Detection (bootstrap/detect_os.sh)
```bash
_needle_detect_os() {
    case "$(uname -s)" in
        Linux*)
            if [[ -f /etc/debian_version ]]; then echo "debian"
            elif [[ -f /etc/redhat-release ]]; then echo "redhat"
            elif [[ -f /etc/arch-release ]]; then echo "arch"
            else echo "linux-unknown"
            fi
            ;;
        Darwin*) echo "macos" ;;
        *) echo "unknown" ;;
    esac
}

_needle_detect_pkg_manager() {
    if command -v apt-get &>/dev/null; then echo "apt"
    elif command -v dnf &>/dev/null; then echo "dnf"
    elif command -v yum &>/dev/null; then echo "yum"
    elif command -v pacman &>/dev/null; then echo "pacman"
    elif command -v brew &>/dev/null; then echo "brew"
    else echo "none"
    fi
}
```

### Installation Methods
```bash
_needle_install_tmux() {
    case "$(_needle_detect_pkg_manager)" in
        apt)    sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y tmux ;;
        dnf)    sudo dnf install -y tmux ;;
        pacman) sudo pacman -S --noconfirm tmux ;;
        brew)   brew install tmux ;;
        *)      _needle_error "Cannot auto-install tmux. Please install manually." ;;
    esac
}

_needle_install_jq() {
    # Similar pattern, with fallback to binary download
    case "$(_needle_detect_pkg_manager)" in
        apt)    sudo apt-get install -y jq ;;
        brew)   brew install jq ;;
        *)
            # Fallback: download binary
            local url="https://github.com/jqlang/jq/releases/latest/download/jq-linux-amd64"
            curl -fsSL "$url" -o "$NEEDLE_CACHE/jq"
            chmod +x "$NEEDLE_CACHE/jq"
            ;;
    esac
}

_needle_install_yq() {
    # yq is often not in package managers, download binary
    local arch=$(uname -m)
    local os=$(uname -s | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')
    local url="https://github.com/mikefarah/yq/releases/latest/download/yq_${os}_${arch}"
    curl -fsSL "$url" -o "$NEEDLE_CACHE/yq"
    chmod +x "$NEEDLE_CACHE/yq"
}

_needle_install_br() {
    # br from beads_rust releases
    local arch=$(uname -m)
    local os=$(uname -s | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')
    local url="https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_rust/releases/latest/download/br-${os}-${arch}"
    curl -fsSL "$url" -o "$NEEDLE_CACHE/br"
    chmod +x "$NEEDLE_CACHE/br"
}
```

### PATH Management
```bash
_needle_setup_path() {
    # Add ~/.local/bin and ~/.needle/cache to PATH
    local dirs=("$HOME/.local/bin" "$NEEDLE_CACHE")
    for dir in "${dirs[@]}"; do
        if [[ ":$PATH:" != *":$dir:"* ]]; then
            export PATH="$dir:$PATH"
            # Also add to shell rc file
            _needle_add_to_shell_rc "$dir"
        fi
    done
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Detects OS correctly (Linux variants, macOS)
- [ ] Detects package manager (apt, dnf, pacman, brew)
- [ ] Installs tmux via package manager

title: Implement main worker loop (runner/loop.sh)
description:
## Overview
Create the main worker loop that processes beads continuously.

## Background & Rationale
The worker loop is the heart of NEEDLE. It:
1. Runs the strand engine to find work
2. Claims and executes beads
3. Handles results (complete/fail)
4. Repeats until stopped

## Technical Requirements

### Main Loop
```bash
_needle_worker_loop() {
    local workspace="$1"
    local agent="$2"
    
    _needle_emit_event "worker.started" \
        "workspace=$workspace" \
        "agent=$agent"
    
    local consecutive_empty=0
    local max_empty=$(_needle_config_get "runner.max_consecutive_empty" 5)
    local polling_interval=$(_needle_config_get "runner.polling_interval" "2s")
    local idle_timeout=$(_needle_config_get "runner.idle_timeout" "300s")
    
    local idle_start=""
    
    while true; do
        # Check for shutdown signal
        if [[ -f "$NEEDLE_STATE/shutdown_$NEEDLE_IDENTIFIER" ]]; then
            _needle_emit_event "worker.draining"
            break
        fi
        
        # Emit heartbeat
        _needle_emit_heartbeat
        
        # Run strand engine to find work
        local result=$(_needle_strand_engine "$workspace" "$agent")
        
        if [[ "$result" == "work_done" ]]; then
            consecutive_empty=0
            idle_start=""
        elif [[ "$result" == "no_work" ]]; then
            ((consecutive_empty++))
            
            # Track idle time
            if [[ -z "$idle_start" ]]; then
                idle_start=$(date +%s)
            fi
            
            local idle_seconds=$(($(date +%s) - idle_start))
            
            _needle_emit_event "worker.idle" \
                "consecutive_empty=$consecutive_empty" \
                "idle_seconds=$idle_seconds"
            
            # Check idle timeout
            if ((idle_seconds >= ${idle_timeout%s})); then
                _needle_emit_event "worker.stopped" "reason=idle_timeout"
                break
            fi
        fi
        
        # Wait before next iteration
        sleep "${polling_interval%s}"
    done
    
    _needle_emit_event "worker.stopped" "reason=shutdown"
}
```

### Bead Processing
```bash
_needle_process_bead() {
    local bead_id="$1"
    local workspace="$2"
    local agent="$3"
    
    _needle_emit_event "bead.claimed" \
        "bead_id=$bead_id" \
        "workspace=$workspace"
    
    # Build prompt
    local prompt=$(_needle_build_prompt "$bead_id" "$workspace")
    local bead_title=$(br show "$bead_id" --json | jq -r '.title')
    
    _needle_emit_event "bead.prompt_built" \
        "bead_id=$bead_id" \
        "prompt_length=${#prompt}"
    
    # Run pre-execute hook
    if ! _needle_run_hook "pre_execute" "$bead_id"; then
        _needle_release_bead "$bead_id" "hook_failed"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Dispatch to agent
    _needle_emit_event "bead.agent_started" \
        "bead_id=$bead_id" \
        "agent=$agent"
    
    local result=$(_needle_dispatch_agent "$agent" "$workspace" "$prompt" "$bead_id" "$bead_title")
    IFS='|' read -r exit_code duration output_file <<< "$result"
    
    _needle_emit_event "bead.agent_completed" \
        "bead_id=$bead_id" \
        "exit_code=$exit_code" \
        "duration_ms=$duration"
    
    # Run post-execute hook
    NEEDLE_EXIT_CODE="$exit_code" \
    NEEDLE_DURATION_MS="$duration" \
    NEEDLE_OUTPUT_FILE="$output_file" \
    _needle_run_hook "post_execute" "$bead_id"
    
    # Handle result
    if [[ "$exit_code" -eq 0 ]]; then
        _needle_complete_bead "$bead_id" "$output_file"
    else
        _needle_fail_bead "$bead_id" "$exit_code" "$output_file"
    fi
    
    # Cleanup
    rm -f "$output_file"
}
```

### Completion/Failure
```bash
_needle_complete_bead() {
    local bead_id="$1"
    local output_file="$2"
    
    # Run pre-complete hook (quality gates)
    if ! _needle_run_hook "pre_complete" "$bead_id"; then
        _needle_fail_bead "$bead_id" "hook_failed"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Mark as completed
    br update "$bead_id" --status closed
    
    _needle_emit_event "bead.completed" \
        "bead_id=$bead_id"
    
    # Run post-complete hook
    _needle_run_hook "post_complete" "$bead_id"
}

_needle_fail_bead() {
    local bead_id="$1"
    local reason="$2"
    local output_file="${3:-}"
    
    # Release back to queue
    br update "$bead_id" --release --reason "$reason"
    
    _needle_emit_event "bead.failed" \
        "bead_id=$bead_id" \
        "reason=$reason"
    
    # Run on_failure hook
    _needle_run_hook "on_failure" "$bead_id"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Loop runs continuously until stopped
- [ ] Calls strand engine for work
- [ ] Claims and processes beads
- [ ] Handles success (close bead)
- [ ] Handles failure (release bead)
- [ ] Emits all lifecycle events
- [ ] Respects polling interval
- [ ] Respects idle timeout
- [ ] Clean shutdown on signal

## Dependencies
- nd-1cz (telemetry events)
- nd-31l (bead claiming)
- nd-1hr (agent dispatcher)

## Labels

description:

## Scope

## Technical Requirements

### Loop Structure
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# runner/loop.sh

main_loop() {
    initialize_worker
    
    while ! should_shutdown; do
        work=  # Calls strand engine
        
        if [[ -n "$work" ]]; then
            execute_work "$work"  # Handled by Part 2
        else
            sleep 5  # No work available
        fi
    done
    
    cleanup_worker
}
```

### Initialization

title: Implement Strand 6: Pulse (strands/pulse.sh)
description:
## Overview
Implement Strand 6 (pulse) for codebase health monitoring and automatic bead generation.

## Background & Rationale
Pulse proactively monitors codebase health and creates beads for discovered issues:
- Security vulnerabilities (CVEs)
- Stale dependencies
- Documentation drift
- Test coverage gaps
- Stale TODOs

## Technical Requirements

### Pulse Configuration
```yaml
strands:
  pulse:
    enabled: false  # Opt-in
    frequency: 24h
    max_beads_per_run: 5
    
    detectors:
      security_scan: true
      dependency_freshness:
        enabled: true
        max_age_days: 90
      doc_drift: true
      coverage_gaps:
        enabled: true
        threshold: 60
      stale_todos:
        enabled: true
        max_age_days: 30
```

### Implementation
```bash
_needle_strand_pulse() {
    local workspace="$1"
    local agent="$2"
    
    # Check frequency
    local state_dir="$NEEDLE_STATE/pulse"
    local last_scan_file="$state_dir/last_scan.json"
    local seen_file="$state_dir/seen_issues.json"
    local frequency=$(_needle_config_get "strands.pulse.frequency" "86400")  # 24h
    
    mkdir -p "$state_dir"
    
    if [[ -f "$last_scan_file" ]]; then
        local last=$(jq -r '.timestamp' "$last_scan_file")
        local last_epoch=$(date -d "$last" +%s 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
        local now=$(date +%s)
        if ((now - last_epoch < frequency)); then
            return 1  # Too soon
        fi
    fi
    
    _needle_emit_event "strand.pulse.started"
    
    # Run detectors
    local detector_output=""
    
    # Security scan (if configured)
    if [[ "$(_needle_config_get "strands.pulse.detectors.security_scan")" == "true" ]]; then
        detector_output+="### Security Scan\n"
        # Use npm audit, pip-audit, or similar
        if [[ -f "$workspace/package.json" ]]; then
            detector_output+="$(cd "$workspace" && npm audit --json 2>/dev/null | jq -c '.' || echo '{}')\n"
        fi
    fi
    
    # Stale TODOs
    if [[ "$(_needle_config_get "strands.pulse.detectors.stale_todos.enabled")" == "true" ]]; then
        local max_age=$(_needle_config_get "strands.pulse.detectors.stale_todos.max_age_days" "30")
        detector_output+="### Stale TODOs\n"
        detector_output+="$(grep -rn 'TODO\|FIXME\|HACK' "$workspace" --include="*.{js,ts,py,go,rs,sh}" 2>/dev/null | head -50 || echo "")\n"
    fi
    
    # Get current open beads (avoid duplicates)
    local open_beads=$(br list --workspace="$workspace" --status open --json 2>/dev/null)
    
    # Build pulse prompt
    local prompt=$(_needle_build_pulse_prompt "$workspace" "$detector_output" "$open_beads")
    
    # Run analysis
    local result=$(_needle_dispatch_agent "$agent" "$workspace" "$prompt" "pulse" "pulse-analysis")
    IFS='|' read -r exit_code duration output_file <<< "$result"
    
    local analysis=$(cat "$output_file")
    rm -f "$output_file"
    
    # Create beads for issues
    local issues=$(echo "$analysis" | jq -c '.issues // []')
    local max_beads=$(_needle_config_get "strands.pulse.max_beads_per_run" "5")
    local created=0
    
    # Load seen issues
    local seen="{}"
    [[ -f "$seen_file" ]] && seen=$(cat "$seen_file")
    
    while IFS= read -r issue && ((created < max_beads)); do
        local title=$(echo "$issue" | jq -r '.title')
        local hash=$(echo "$title" | md5sum | cut -c1-8)
        
        # Skip if already seen
        if echo "$seen" | jq -e ".\"$hash\"" &>/dev/null; then
            continue
        fi
        
        local description=$(echo "$issue" | jq -r '.description')
        local severity=$(echo "$issue" | jq -r '.severity // "medium"')
        local priority=2
        [[ "$severity" == "critical" ]] && priority=0
        [[ "$severity" == "high" ]] && priority=1
        
        br create "$title" \
            --workspace="$workspace" \
            --description "$description" \
            --priority "$priority" \
            --type task \
            --label "pulse-generated" \
            --label "severity-$severity"
        
        # Mark as seen
        seen=$(echo "$seen" | jq --arg h "$hash" --arg t "$title" '. + {($h): $t}')
        
        ((created++))
        
        _needle_emit_event "pulse.issue_created" \
            "title=$title" \
            "severity=$severity"
    done < <(echo "$issues" | jq -c '.[]')
    
    # Save state
    echo "$seen" > "$seen_file"
    jq -nc --arg ts "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)" --arg count "$created" \
        '{timestamp: $ts, issues_created: ($count | tonumber)}' > "$last_scan_file"
    
    _needle_emit_event "strand.pulse.completed" \
        "issues_created=$created"
    
    if ((created > 0)); then
        echo "work_done"
        return 0
    fi
    
    return 1
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Respects frequency limit
- [ ] Runs configured detectors
- [ ] Avoids duplicate issues (seen tracking)
- [ ] Creates beads with severity-based priority
- [ ] Respects max_beads_per_run
- [ ] Saves state for deduplication
- [ ] Emits pulse events
- [ ] Opt-in only

## Dependencies
- nd-hpj (strand engine)
- nd-1hr (agent dispatcher)

## Labels
phase-3, strands, pulse

title: Implement needle run command (cli/run.sh)
description:
## Overview
Implement the `needle run` command that starts workers to process beads.

## Background & Rationale
This is the primary command users invoke to start NEEDLE workers. It:
- Creates a tmux session (self-invoking)
- Configures the worker
- Starts the main loop

## Technical Requirements

### Command Options
As documented in plan.md:
```
needle run [OPTIONS]

OPTIONS:
    -w, --workspace <PATH>   Workspace directory
    -a, --agent <NAME>       Agent adapter to use
    -i, --id <ID>            Worker identifier
    -c, --count <N>          Number of workers to spawn
    --no-tmux                Run without tmux
    --foreground             Run in foreground
    --strands <LIST>         Comma-separated strands
    -h, --help               Print help
```

### Implementation
```bash
_needle_run() {
    local workspace=""
    local agent=""
    local identifier=""
    local count=1
    local no_tmux=false
    local foreground=false
    local strands=""
    local _in_tmux=false
    
    # Parse options
    while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
        case "$1" in
            -w|--workspace) workspace="$2"; shift 2 ;;
            -a|--agent) agent="$2"; shift 2 ;;
            -i|--id) identifier="$2"; shift 2 ;;
            -c|--count) count="$2"; shift 2 ;;
            --no-tmux) no_tmux=true; shift ;;
            --foreground) foreground=true; shift ;;
            --strands) strands="$2"; shift 2 ;;
            --_tmux) _in_tmux=true; shift ;;
            -h|--help) _needle_run_help; return 0 ;;
            *) _needle_error "Unknown option: $1"; return 1 ;;
        esac
    done
    
    # Defaults from config
    workspace="${workspace:-$(_needle_config_get 'workspaces[0]' '.')}"
    agent="${agent:-$(_needle_config_get 'default_agent' '')}"
    
    # Validate
    if [[ -z "$agent" ]]; then
        _needle_error "No agent specified. Use --agent or set default_agent in config."
        return 1
    fi
    
    if [[ ! -d "$workspace" ]]; then
        _needle_error "Workspace not found: $workspace"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Validate agent
    if ! _needle_validate_agent "$agent"; then
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Parse agent for naming
    IFS='-' read -r runner provider model <<< "$agent"
    
    # Generate identifier if not provided
    identifier="${identifier:-$(_needle_next_identifier "$runner" "$provider" "$model")}"
    
    # Generate session name
    local pattern=$(_needle_config_get 'naming.pattern' 'needle-{runner}-{provider}-{model}-{identifier}')
    local session=$(_needle_generate_session_name "$pattern" "$runner" "$provider" "$model" "$identifier")
    
    # Export for use in worker
    export NEEDLE_SESSION="$session"
    export NEEDLE_RUNNER="$runner"
    export NEEDLE_PROVIDER="$provider"
    export NEEDLE_MODEL="$model"
    export NEEDLE_IDENTIFIER="$identifier"
    export NEEDLE_WORKSPACE="$workspace"
    export NEEDLE_AGENT="$agent"
    export NEEDLE_LOG_FILE="$NEEDLE_LOGS/${session}.jsonl"
    
    # Handle multiple workers
    if ((count > 1)); then
        for ((i=1; i<=count; i++)); do
            "$0" run --workspace="$workspace" --agent="$agent" &
        done
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Self-invoke into tmux if needed
    if ! $_in_tmux && ! $no_tmux && ! _needle_in_tmux; then
        _needle_self_invoke_tmux "$session" run --workspace="$workspace" --agent="$agent" --id="$identifier"
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Actually run the worker
    _needle_ensure_dirs
    mkdir -p "$(dirname "$NEEDLE_LOG_FILE")"
    
    _needle_info "Starting worker $identifier..."
    _needle_info "Session: $session"
    _needle_info "Workspace: $workspace"
    _needle_info "Agent: $agent"
    
    # Start the loop
    _needle_worker_loop "$workspace" "$agent"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Parses all command options correctly
- [ ] Defaults from config work
- [ ] Validates agent exists
- [ ] Validates workspace exists
- [ ] Generates session name correctly
- [ ] Auto-generates NATO identifier
- [ ] Self-invokes into tmux
- [ ] --no-tmux runs directly
- [ ] --count=N spawns multiple workers
- [ ] Exports all environment variables
- [ ] Creates log file directory
- [ ] Starts worker loop

## Dependencies
- nd-u74 (tmux management)
- nd-qft (worker loop)
- nd-3pu (agent loader)

## Labels
phase-1, cli, run

title: Implement tmux session management (runner/tmux.sh)
description:
## Overview
Create the tmux session management module for self-invoking tmux workers.

## Background & Rationale
NEEDLE runs workers in tmux sessions for:
- Persistence across SSH disconnects
- Easy attach/detach for monitoring
- Clean process isolation
- Graceful shutdown support

The self-invoking pattern: `needle run` creates a tmux session and re-execs itself inside it.

## Technical Requirements

### Session Detection
```bash
_needle_in_tmux() {
    [[ -n "${TMUX:-}" ]]
}

_needle_session_exists() {
    local session="$1"
    tmux has-session -t "$session" 2>/dev/null
}
```

### Session Creation
```bash
_needle_create_session() {
    local session="$1"
    shift
    local cmd="$*"
    
    # Create detached session running our command
    tmux new-session -d -s "$session" "$cmd"
}

_needle_self_invoke_tmux() {
    local session="$1"
    
    if _needle_in_tmux; then
        # Already in tmux, run directly
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Build command to re-exec ourselves with --_tmux flag
    local cmd="$0 $@ --_tmux"
    
    # Create session
    _needle_create_session "$session" "$cmd"
    
    _needle_info "Worker started in tmux session: $session"
    _needle_info "Attach with: needle attach $session"
    
    # We're done (the actual work happens in the new session)
    return 0
}
```

### Session Naming
```bash
_needle_generate_session_name() {
    local pattern="${1:-needle-{runner}-{provider}-{model}-{identifier}}"
    local runner="$2"
    local provider="$3"
    local model="$4"
    local identifier="$5"
    
    local name="$pattern"
    name="${name//\{runner\}/$runner}"
    name="${name//\{provider\}/$provider}"
    name="${name//\{model\}/$model}"
    name="${name//\{identifier\}/$identifier}"
    
    echo "$name"
}
```

### NATO Alphabet Naming
```bash
_needle_next_identifier() {
    local runner="$1"
    local provider="$2"
    local model="$3"
    
    local prefix="needle-$runner-$provider-$model-"
    
    # Find existing sessions with this prefix
    local existing=$(tmux list-sessions -F '#{session_name}' 2>/dev/null | grep "^$prefix" || true)
    
    # Find first unused NATO name
    for name in "${NEEDLE_NATO_ALPHABET[@]}"; do
        if ! echo "$existing" | grep -q "^${prefix}${name}$"; then
            echo "$name"
            return 0
        fi
    done
    
    # All NATO names used, fall back to number
    echo "$(($(echo "$existing" | wc -l) + 1))"
}
```

### Session Operations
```bash
_needle_attach_session() {
    local session="$1"
    local read_only="${2:-false}"
    
    if $read_only; then
        tmux attach-session -t "$session" -r
    else
        tmux attach-session -t "$session"
    fi
}

_needle_kill_session() {
    local session="$1"
    tmux kill-session -t "$session"
}

_needle_list_sessions() {
    tmux list-sessions -F '#{session_name}|#{session_created}|#{session_attached}' 2>/dev/null | \
        grep '^needle-' || true
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Detects if running inside tmux
- [ ] Creates detached tmux session
- [ ] Self-invokes into tmux correctly
- [ ] Session naming follows pattern
- [ ] NATO alphabet identifier generation
- [ ] Attach/detach works correctly
- [ ] Session listing filters to needle- prefix
- [ ] Kill session terminates cleanly

## Dependencies
- nd-1il (shared libraries)

## Labels
phase-1, runner, tmux

title: Implement needle status command (cli/status.sh)
description:
## Overview
Implement the `needle status` command for dashboard view.

## Technical Requirements

### Output

```bash
_needle_status() {
    local watch=false
    local json_output=false
    
    while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
        case "$1" in
            -w|--watch) watch=true; shift ;;
            -j|--json) json_output=true; shift ;;
            -h|--help) _needle_status_help; return 0 ;;
            *) shift ;;
        esac
    done
    
    if $watch; then
        while true; do
            clear
            _needle_status_display "$json_output"
            sleep 2
        done
    else
        _needle_status_display "$json_output"
    fi
}
```
